acceptance criteria: Every planned artifact exists under artifacts/chapter-11 and is nonblank.
All numeric residuals are below the stated tolerances.
The final sanity JSON records the source span, artifacts, libraries, and checks.
chapter goal: Teach hyperbolic geometry through computable model changes, geodesics, isometries, parallels, angle defect, and cross-ratio distance.
computational checks: artifact existence and nonzero file size
triangle angle defect positivity and hyperbolic cosine rule residual
symbolic cross-ratio invariance under a generic Mobius map
concept inventory: Upper half-plane metric is conformal to the Euclidean plane but changes length by height.
Hyperbolic lines in H are vertical rays and semicircles orthogonal to the real boundary.
The Cayley map moves H to the Poincare disk and transfers distances, angles, and geodesics.
Real Mobius maps with positive determinant act as orientation-preserving isometries of H.
Horocycles and equidistant curves are not hyperbolic lines, even when Euclidean coordinates make them look simple.
Nonintersecting lines split into limiting parallel and ultra-parallel behavior.
Hyperbolic triangle area is angle defect; similar triangles are rigid rather than scaleable.
The Beltrami-Klein model straightens geodesics but loses conformality; distance becomes a cross-ratio.
implementation notes: All outputs are written under the chapter-11 artifact subtree.
Matplotlib is used for durable 2D geometric diagrams; Plotly is reserved for the parameterized Mobius flow; SymPy checks the projective invariant exactly.
No shared utilities or indexes are edited.

library routing table.fallback: plain SVG would work but would make numeric checks less direct
library routing table.why: The models are planar and the teaching target is incidence, orthogonality, and labeled arcs.

library routing table.fallback: a static before/after Matplotlib panel is also saved
library routing table.why: The parameter changes continuously, and an HTML artifact lets learners inspect the orbit without rerunning code.

library routing table.fallback: numeric random tests only, with weaker assurance
library routing table.representation: exact symbolic identity plus numeric Klein distance check
library routing table.why: The projective invariant is algebraic, while the distance comparison is numeric.

proof-visualization strategy: Use invariant trackers rather than copied proof diagrams: distance preservation under isometries, angle-defect area, limiting-parallel angle, and cross-ratio invariance expose the proof state numerically or symbolically.
risks: Plotly HTML display depends on notebook frontend support, so a static Mobius panel is also saved.
Angles in the Poincare disk are measured with sampled geodesic tangents; tolerances are explicit.

source span read.book: Andrew Pressley, Elementary Differential Geometry, Second Edition
source span read.use: orientation only; no copied prose, exercises, screenshots, page crops, or figures

visual sequence.learner inspection target: semicircles and vertical rays in H become disk arcs orthogonal to the unit circle
geodesics map to geodesics while pairwise distances stay fixed
visual sequence.representation: before/after H plot plus Plotly disk flow
visual sequence.validation/invariant: maximum pairwise distance residual under the Mobius map

visual sequence.learner inspection target: fixed-distance rays and boundary-tangent horocycles are Euclidean-simple but not geodesics
visual sequence.validation/invariant: asinh(|v|/w) is constant along each ray

visual sequence.learner inspection target: only two nonintersecting geodesics through the point share an ideal endpoint with the line
visual sequence.representation: Poincare disk limiting parallels through a point

visual sequence.learner inspection target: angle sum is below pi and the defect is the area
visual sequence.representation: conformal disk triangle with measured angles

visual sequence.learner inspection target: the same geodesic is curved in Poincare and straight in Klein
visual sequence.representation: Poincare arc compared with Klein chord
